title: One Painting, Many Photos
summary: At a museum in Madrid, two friends disagree about how to really see art.

body.speaker: Noah
body.text: Priya, we are in this big museum in Madrid. There are two hundred paintings. Come on, we must see them all!

body.speaker: Priya
body.text: Wait. I want to stay here. I like this one painting. It is a small blue boat on the sea.

body.speaker: Noah
body.text: But it is just one painting. I take a photo of it. Let's go to the next room.

body.speaker: Priya
body.text: A photo is fast. But I do not see the painting in a photo. I want to look slowly. The sea is not just blue. It is green here, and grey there.

body.speaker: Noah
body.text: Hmm. I do not see that. I take the photo fast. Maybe I look at my phone more than the painting.

body.speaker: Priya
body.text: Yes! When you take a photo, you stop looking. The photo says, 'I will look later.' But later never comes.

body.speaker: Noah
body.text: That is true. I have many photos on my phone. I never open them. So I see two hundred paintings, but I remember nothing.

body.speaker: Priya
body.text: And I see one painting, but I remember it for years. I see the small white bird on the boat. I see the old man's hat.

body.speaker: Noah
body.text: But Priya, your way is slow. We only see five paintings today, not two hundred. Is that not a little sad?

body.speaker: Priya
body.text: Maybe. Your way is wide, and my way is deep. You see many things a little. Both ways have something good.

body.speaker: Noah
body.text: Okay. Today, let's try your way. I put my phone in my bag. Now, show me the green and grey sea.

annotations.meaningVi: một con thuyền nhỏ màu xanh
cái lúc 'sau này' chẳng bao giờ tới

grammarNotes.title: must for need
grammarNotes.bodyVi: "must" diễn tả điều cần thiết hoặc bắt buộc.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Noah says "we must see them all" to show a strong need.

grammarNotes.title: Comparison: wide and deep
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Có thể dùng tính từ để so sánh hai cách làm khác nhau.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Priya says "Your way is wide, and my way is deep," comparing two ways of looking.

grammarNotes.title: Present simple for general truths
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì hiện tại đơn nói về điều luôn đúng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: See "When you take a photo, you stop looking" — a general truth.

translationVi:
Noah: Priya, chúng ta đang ở trong bảo tàng lớn này ở Madrid. Có hai trăm bức tranh. Đi nào, chúng ta phải xem hết tất cả!
Priya: Khoan đã. Tớ muốn ở lại đây. Tớ thích bức tranh này. Đó là một con thuyền nhỏ màu xanh trên biển.
Noah: Nhưng nó chỉ là một bức tranh. Tớ chụp một tấm ảnh nó. Sang phòng tiếp theo thôi.
Priya: Chụp ảnh thì nhanh. Nhưng tớ không nhìn thấy bức tranh trong một tấm ảnh. Tớ muốn nhìn chậm rãi. Biển không chỉ màu xanh đâu. Chỗ này xanh lục, chỗ kia xám.
Noah: Hừm. Tớ không thấy điều đó. Tớ chụp ảnh thật nhanh. Có lẽ tớ nhìn điện thoại nhiều hơn nhìn bức tranh.
Priya: Đúng vậy! Khi cậu chụp ảnh, cậu ngừng nhìn. Tấm ảnh nói: "Để sau xem." Nhưng cái lúc 'sau này' chẳng bao giờ tới.
Noah: Đúng thật. Tớ có nhiều tấm ảnh trong điện thoại. Tớ chẳng bao giờ mở chúng ra. Vậy nên tớ xem hai trăm bức tranh, mà chẳng nhớ gì.
Priya: Còn tớ xem một bức tranh, nhưng tớ nhớ nó nhiều năm. Tớ thấy con chim nhỏ màu trắng trên thuyền. Tớ thấy cái mũ của ông già.
Noah: Nhưng Priya, cách của cậu chậm lắm. Hôm nay chúng ta chỉ xem được năm bức tranh, chứ không phải hai trăm. Như vậy chẳng phải hơi buồn sao?
Priya: Có lẽ. Cách của cậu thì rộng, cách của tớ thì sâu. Cậu thấy nhiều thứ một chút. Cả hai cách đều có điều gì đó tốt.
Noah: Được rồi. Hôm nay, thử cách của cậu nhé. Tớ cất điện thoại vào túi. Nào, cho tớ xem vùng biển xanh lục và xám đi.

questions.explanation: "We are in this big museum in Madrid."

questions.prompt: How many paintings are in the museum?
What is in the painting Priya likes?
options: A small blue boat on the sea
questions.explanation: "It is a small blue boat on the sea."

questions.prompt: What does Noah do instead of looking long?
questions.explanation: "I take a photo of it. Now we have it."

questions.prompt: What does Priya notice in the sea?
options: It is green here and grey there, not just blue
questions.explanation: "It is green here, and grey there."
questions.hint: Look at Priya's line about looking slowly.

questions.prompt: What does Priya say happens when you take a photo?
questions.explanation: "When you take a photo, you stop looking."
questions.hint: Look at Priya's line about 'I will look later.'

options: He sees many paintings but never opens his photos
questions.explanation: "I see two hundred paintings, but I remember nothing."
questions.hint: Look at Noah's line about a thousand photos.

questions.prompt: What small things does Priya see in the painting?
options: A white bird and an old man's hat
A red flag and a dog
questions.explanation: "I see the small white bird on the boat. I see the old man's hat."
questions.hint: Look at Priya's line about details.

questions.prompt: What does Noah say is a problem with Priya's way?
options: It is slow, so they see few paintings
questions.explanation: "Your way is slow. We only see five paintings today."
questions.hint: Look at Noah's line near the end.

questions.prompt: How does Priya describe the two ways?
options: His way is short, her way is tall
His way is hot, her way is cold
His way is wide, her way is deep
questions.explanation: "Your way is wide, and my way is deep."
questions.hint: Look at Priya's last long line.

questions.prompt: What does Noah do at the very end?
options: He puts his phone in his bag and tries her way
questions.explanation: "I put my phone in my bag. Now, show me the green and grey sea."

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of this talk?
options: Looking slowly and looking widely each have value
questions.hint: Think about the whole conversation, not one line.

cloze.template:
Noah: Priya, we are in this big {{b1}} in Madrid. There are two hundred paintings. Come on, we must see them all!
Priya: Wait. I want to stay here. I like this one painting. It is a small blue {{b2}} on the sea.
Noah: But it is just one painting. I take a {{b3}} of it. Let's go to the next room.
Priya: A photo is fast. But I do not see the painting in a photo. I want to look {{b4}}. The sea is not just blue. It is green here, and {{b5}} there.
Noah: Hmm. I do not see that. I take the photo fast. Maybe I look at my {{b6}} more than the painting.
Priya: Yes! When you take a photo, you stop {{b7}}. The photo says, 'I will look later.' But later never comes.
Noah: That is true. I have many photos on my phone. I never open them. So I see two hundred paintings, but I remember {{b8}}.
Priya: And I see one painting, but I remember it for years. I see the small white {{b9}} on the boat. I see the old man's hat.
Noah: But Priya, your way is slow. We only see five paintings today, not two hundred. Is that not a little sad?
Priya: Maybe. Your way is {{b10}}, and my way is {{b11}}. You see many things a little. Both ways have something good.
Noah: Okay. Today, let's try your way. I put my phone in my {{b12}}. Now, show me the green and grey sea.

blanks.explanation: They are in a big museum.
It is a small blue boat on the sea.
Noah takes a photo of it.
The sea is green here and grey there.
Noah looks at his phone more than the painting.
When you take a photo, you stop looking.
He sees many paintings but remembers nothing.
She sees the small white bird on the boat.
He puts his phone in his bag.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Priya says "When you take a photo, you stop looking." Think of the last photo you took. Did you really look at the thing, or only at your phone?
